name: Salmon & Shrimp Spring Rolls
recipeInstructions:
1. In a fairly large saucepan or frying pan, sauté garlic, scallions, and carrot in a little oil until the vegetables are limp. Add wine (dry sherry), soy sauce, spinach, seafood seasoning, mushrooms, and shrimp, stirring constantly while breaking up the salmon into small pieces. Be careful not to overcook; shrimp should turn pink, and mushrooms and spinach should have rendered their liquid and become soft. Allow this mixture to cool before assembling the rolls.
2. Lay out a sheet of wax paper for a work area, and keep a saucer with water nearby. It's best to prepare three rolls at a time. Place about a rounded teaspoon of filling in the lower third of each wonton wrapper. Fold the two sides over the mixture halfway, then moisten the top end of the wrapper with water from the saucer. Roll up and seal the roll. For frying, heat enough oil in a pan to cover the rolls. Fry for 3-5 minutes on each side until golden brown. You can fry 5-8 rolls at a time depending on the size of the pan; turn them occasionally to check doneness and color. Drain on paper towels.
Dipping Sauce:
Mix 2 tablespoons Grey Poupon mustard with 1 teaspoon honey and 1 tablespoon dark soy sauce.
